phases.name: System Development Phase
phases.description: The system development phase of the Tinymachines project ecosystem saw the creation of a small knowledge net, a vector comparison tool in C++, and various system utilities such as screen session management and Linux process viewing. This phase focused on building foundational systems for the project.

phases.name: Initial Setup Phase
phases.description: The first week of development saw the creation of basic projects to establish the foundation for the Tinymachines project ecosystem. A variety of tools and applications were built, including a random number generator, a tiny bug tracker, and a simple HTML visualization. These early milestones set the stage for further development.

phases.name: Tardus Research and Development
phases.description: This phase saw the creation of various tools and platforms for research and development, including EEG monitor experimentation platform, a bootstrap guide for learning assembly for the ARM64 on a Raspberry Pi 5, and several programming languages and projects such as Tardus, an agentic programming language designed for self-improving systems.

milestones.title: Bootstrap guide for learning assembly for the ARM64 on a Raspberry Pi 5

phases.name: Initial Core Setup
phases.description: The initial core setup phase involved creating the foundation for the Tinymachines project ecosystem. This included setting up the repository structure, installing dependencies, and defining the core components of each system.

milestones.title: Initial project setup with structure and dependencies
Fix KeyError and add Ollama test harness

phases.name: Initial Project Setup and Prototyping
phases.description: This phase saw the creation of seven initial projects as part of the tinymachines project ecosystem. The projects were developed in various programming languages, including TypeScript, Python, JavaScript, HTML, C, and an unknown language. The primary focus was on setting up the foundation for future development.

phases.name: Alpha Development Phase
phases.description: The Alpha Development Phase of the tinymachines project ecosystem was characterized by rapid experimentation and prototyping. During this phase, seven core repositories were created to lay the groundwork for a comprehensive machine learning and computer vision framework. Key milestones included the development of interactive command-line interfaces, macro systems, and database analytics.

milestones.title: v1.0.0 Release: Macro System, Interactive Mode, and Syntax Highlighting

phases.name: Early Prototyping Phase
phases.description: The Early Prototyping Phase saw the initial creation of key components in the Tinymachines project ecosystem, including the 'resume' repository with its basic setup and files upload functionality.

milestones.title: Add 6502 Processor Expert Agent with RAG System
Scout Network Scanner Shell Repository Created

phases.name: Refinement and Expansion
phases.description: After establishing a foundation with the initial repos, the team expanded the project by adding features to deox, gnomes, collidr, spicy, orator, and esp32. The refactoring and polishing phase focused on refining the user experience, improving system efficiency, and enhancing AI capabilities.

repos.name: scout
repos.description: The Scout network scanner is a shell-based tool that performs various tasks such as automated key installation, drive-time guide, run verification report, and more. The repository has undergone significant updates since its initial release on November 22, 2025, with recent commits adding features like SSH access enumeration, distributed deployment system, and service reconnaissance system. The current version is v1.0.0.
